rationale: The citing paper uses Soman and Gourville (2001) as a core theoretical input in hypothesis development, not just as background. In the theory section, it explicitly draws on transaction decoupling to argue that vouchers add an intermediary step that weakens the perceived coupling between focal-product cost and gift benefit, which drives its predicted effects on purchase intention. The paper also revisits this citation in the discussion to position its findings as contributing to the transaction-decoupling stream.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the transaction-decoupling idea from Soman and Gourville (2001) to conditional promotions, arguing that vouchers decouple payment from gift receipt and reduce value comparison. It uses this framework to derive and interpret its voucher-versus-direct-gift effects.
